name: Playa Pita Loop Trail
name_original: Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular
region: Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo
description: Easy 2.6-mile (4.2 km) circular hike around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, featuring reservoir views and a submerged bell tower.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are set within a diverse landscape in the province of Soria, Spain. The region is characterized by the expansive reservoir, surrounded by dense pine forests (Pinares de Soria) and oak groves. The terrain includes mountainous backdrops from the Picos de Urbión, offering a mix of waterside paths and forest trails. Best waterfall hiking trails around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo The most popular waterfall hiking route is Cascadas de Covaleda – circular, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete, leading through forested areas to a series of cascades. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ojeda River Waterfall – Paso Peñoncito Waterfall loop from Paso de Frigidillas, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route offers views of the Ojeda River and its waterfalls, traversing varied forest and riverside terrain. Local hikers also love the Paso Peñoncito Waterfall – Ojeda River Waterfall loop from Paso de Frigidillas, a 2.3 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through woodlands and along the Ojeda River, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Hiking around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo is defined by pine forests, river valleys, and the reservoir's extensive shoreline. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all 4 trails in this guide rated as moderate. The routes in Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are available around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo?
A: There are 4 dedicated waterfall hiking trails around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, offering diverse experiences through the region's natural beauty.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of waterfall hikes in this area?
A: All the waterfall hiking trails in this guide are rated as &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt;. They offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility, suitable for hikers with some experience.

Q: What makes the landscape around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo unique for waterfall hiking?
A: The region is characterized by the expansive Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo reservoir, surrounded by dense pine forests (Pinares de Soria) and oak groves. The mountainous backdrop of the Picos de Urbión adds to the dramatic scenery, creating a diverse environment for exploring waterfalls amidst waterside paths and forest trails.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall routes available?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057607362/cascadas-de-covaleda-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascadas de Covaleda – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 7 km loop that takes you through forested areas to a series of cascades. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10462886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso Peñoncito Waterfall – Ojeda River Waterfall loop from Paso de Frigidillas&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 3.6 km trail.

Q: What can I expect to see along the waterfall hiking trails?
A: Beyond the waterfalls themselves, you'll encounter the stunning natural features of the region, including the vast reservoir, pine and oak forests, and river valleys. Depending on your chosen route, you might also pass by charming villages or catch glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: What do other hikers say about the waterfall trails here?
A: The waterfall trails around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the natural beauty, the well-maintained paths, and the refreshing experience of discovering the cascades.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions near the waterfall routes?
A: While hiking the waterfall trails, you're in a region rich with points of interest. You might consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt;, a unique sandy beach within a pine forest, or exploring the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2395344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;, a charming settlement. The iconic Submerged Bell Tower of La Muedra, which occasionally rises from the reservoir, is also a notable historical sight in the wider area.

Q: What is the longest waterfall hike in this guide?
A: The longest waterfall hike featured is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17543605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall – Paso Peñoncito Waterfall loop from Paso de Frigidillas&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 9.8 km (6.1 miles) and typically taking around 3 hours to complete.

Q: What is the shortest waterfall hike in this guide?
A: The shortest waterfall hike listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10462886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso Peñoncito Waterfall – Ojeda River Waterfall loop from Paso de Frigidillas&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.6 km (2.3 miles) long and can be completed in approximately 1 hour 24 minutes.

Q: Is the area suitable for a full day of outdoor activities beyond just hiking?
A: Absolutely. The Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo is a hub for various outdoor activities. Besides hiking, you can enjoy water sports like windsurfing, sailing, canoeing, and fishing on the reservoir. The presence of sandy beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt; also makes it ideal for picnics and relaxation.

Q: Are there any other waterfall routes that explore the Ojeda River?
A: Yes, besides the loop from Paso de Frigidillas, you can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall – Paso Peñoncito Waterfall loop from Covaleda&lt;/a&gt;. This route is similar in length, at about 9.6 km, and offers another perspective of the Ojeda River's cascades.
tips: Beautiful views of the Cuerda del Pozo reservoir from Playa Pita, where we will find a parking lot and a recreational area with various services, where we can take a bath and eat.
Path that runs parallel to the Bajero River, which feeds the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir. A very pleasant walk, enjoying the vegetation that surrounds the reservoir.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular route is an easy hike that typically takes about 1 hour and 5 minutes to complete. This estimate is for continuous walking without extended breaks.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific 4.15 km circular route is rated as easy, with minimal elevation gain (around 34 meters). It features easily-accessible paths, making it suitable for beginners and families looking for a pleasant outdoor experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this easy route generally consists of easily-accessible paths. While much of the area features natural, unpaved trails through pine forests and oak groves, some sections might be less manicured. It's always a good idea to wear sturdy shoes for comfort and stability.

faqs.question: What interesting sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll enjoy impressive panoramic views of the expansive blue waters of the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir, surrounded by green pine forests and mountains. You'll also pass by Playa Pita, a popular sandy beach. Keep an eye out for the submerged bell tower of the old church of La Muedra, which becomes visible when water levels are low, offering a unique historical vista.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo area is beautiful year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, while summer is popular for combining hiking with water activities at spots like Playa Pita. Winter can offer a serene, quieter experience, especially if you're hoping to see the submerged village ruins when water levels are typically at their lowest.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trails around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near recreational zones like beaches, to ensure a safe and enjoyable experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is available at various points around the reservoir, particularly near popular recreational areas like Playa Pita. These spots often serve as convenient starting points for circular routes. Arriving earlier in the day, especially during peak season, is advisable to secure a good parking spot.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the hiking trails around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, including this circular route. The natural area is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, the reservoir is a significant recreational hub, especially in summer. You can find amenities and sometimes cafes or restaurants near popular spots like Playa Pita. For more options, the nearby towns will offer a wider selection of dining and accommodation.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this easy circular hike, we recommend bringing sturdy walking shoes, water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are advisable. Don't forget your camera to capture the impressive views and unique features like the submerged bell tower!

faqs.question: What is the 'insider tip' for experiencing the unique historical features of the reservoir?
faqs.answer: To witness the most compelling historical features, such as the bell tower of the submerged village of La Muedra and potentially even the ancient Roman bridge, plan your visit during periods of low water levels. This typically occurs in late summer or autumn, or after prolonged dry spells, when the ruins become more visible and sometimes even accessible to walk upon.
